title: Bound and decouple broadcast fan-out
description: Broadcasting to many subscribers runs concurrently with per-connection isolation so one slow client cannot stall the rest.
body: Use when one event must reach many connected clients. Do not loop subscribers and write synchronously so a slow socket blocks delivery to everyone behind it. Actions: hand each connection its own bounded queue and writer, fan out by enqueuing (non-blocking), and drop/coalesce per the backpressure policy on slow connections; cap total subscribers per channel. Validation signals include a fan-out test where one artificially slow client does not delay others and total memory stays bounded. Risk boundary: synchronous fan-out that head-of-line-blocks on the slowest client degrades the whole channel and should be flagged.
